mentions.note: Rallying on a 4% yield and optimism around the new CEO.

Caller Garrett owns ~300 shares; Cramer says hold on to it; new CEO, 4% yield, bouncing off lows for first time in long time; willing to bless owning it, maybe even buy another 100 if it hits $100; wants to have new CEO on show.
Up 4% on strong ISM triggering institutional retail buying; new leadership underway.
New CEO Mike Fidella must rapidly reinvent after Canadian division exit; lacks Amazon/Walmart/Costco scale; wait for execution results.
Up 6.75% on strong quarter, new CEO welcome; once-worried dividend now looks safe; retailer winner on oil spike.

New CEO Fideli turnaround credible: Q4 margins expanded despite flat comps, non-merchandise revenue soared, $2B investment plan addresses operational failures; trading 15x vs. Walmart 44x, Costco 50x; proof required but early momentum strong.
Up 18% YTD despite Iran war onset; solid start for new CEO Michael Fideli; consumer resilience holding.
Down in rally; retail complex showing weakness.
Had run hard pre-war, now experiencing huge reversal; profit-taking understandable but part of broader retail weakness from war impact on consumer.
New management reversing performance trends; approaching favorable comparisons after three-month surge; terrific results expected.
Turnaround story with 32% earnings growth; SSS +5.6% (vs 2.4% expected); raised full-year outlook; cheap at 15x earnings with 3.6% yield; home run quarter.
